description: Windows desktop application for converting Kinect V2 .XEF files to JPEG format

features.description: Application window launches successfully
steps: Verify window title shows 'XEF2JPEG - Kinect V2 to JPEG Converter'

features.description: Input file entry field is displayed and editable
steps: Verify entry field is present next to label
Click on entry field and type a test path
Verify text appears in the field

features.description: Output directory entry field is displayed with default value
steps: Verify entry field shows default path ending with 'XEF2JPEG_Output'

features.description: Input file Browse button opens file dialog
steps: Click 'Browse...' button next to Input XEF File field
Verify dialog title shows 'Select XEF File'
Verify file type filter shows XEF files

features.description: File dialog defaults to current working directory
steps: Launch application from a known directory
Verify file dialog opens to the current working directory

features.description: Selected input file path appears in entry field
steps: Verify file path appears in input entry field

features.description: Output directory Browse button opens directory dialog
steps: Click 'Browse...' button next to Output Directory field
Verify dialog title shows 'Select Output Directory'

features.description: Selected output directory path appears in entry field
steps: Verify folder path appears in output directory entry field

features.description: Start Conversion button is displayed
steps: Verify button is visible and enabled

features.description: Progress bar is displayed in initial state
steps: Verify progress bar shows empty/initial state

features.description: Status label shows 'Ready' on launch
Conversion fails with error when no input file is selected
steps: Verify error message says 'Please select an input XEF file.'

features.description: Conversion fails with error when no output directory is selected
steps: Verify error message says 'Please select an output directory.'

features.description: Conversion fails when input file does not exist
steps: Type a non-existent file path in input field
Verify error message says 'Input file does not exist.'

features.description: Valid XEF file conversion completes successfully
steps: Place a valid .xef file in XEF2JPEG_Input folder
Select the .xef file using Browse button
Verify output directory is set to XEF2JPEG_Output
Verify JPEG files are created in output directory

features.description: Progress bar animates during conversion
steps: Set up valid input file and output directory
Verify progress bar stops when conversion completes

features.description: Status shows 'Converting...' during conversion
steps: Set up valid input file and output directory
Verify status returns to 'Ready' after completion

features.description: Convert button is disabled during conversion
steps: Set up valid input file and output directory
Verify button becomes enabled again after conversion

features.description: Output JPEG file has correct image format
steps: Open the output JPEG file in an image viewer
Verify it opens successfully as a JPEG image

features.description: Multiple frames from XEF file are converted
steps: Use an XEF file with multiple color frames
Verify multiple JPEG files are created
Verify each file represents a different frame

features.description: Converted JPEG files have sequential naming
steps: Convert an XEF file with multiple frames
Verify files are named with sequential numbers or frame indices

features.description: Depth data is preserved or colorized in output
steps: Use an XEF file with depth frame data
Verify depth data is represented in output (grayscale or colorized)

features.description: Application handles missing Kinect SDK gracefully
steps: Run application on a system without Kinect SDK installed
Attempt to convert an XEF file
Verify informative error message about missing SDK

features.description: Application stores last used input directory
steps: Browse and select a file from a specific folder
Verify dialog opens to the previously used directory

features.description: Application stores last used output directory
steps: Change output directory to a custom path
Verify output directory shows the previously selected path

features.description: Application has correct window title
steps: Verify title bar shows 'XEF2JPEG - Kinect V2 to JPEG Converter'

features.description: Application window has appropriate default size
steps: Verify window width is approximately 600 pixels
Verify window height is approximately 400 pixels

features.description: Window can be resized by user
steps: Verify UI elements reflow or maintain layout

features.description: Title label is displayed with correct text
steps: Verify it appears at the top of the window
Verify it uses a larger or bold font

features.description: Input label text is correct
steps: Verify label text is exactly 'Input XEF File:'

features.description: Output label text is correct
steps: Verify label text is exactly 'Output Directory:'

features.description: Browse buttons display correct text
steps: Verify both Browse buttons show 'Browse...' text

features.description: Start Conversion button displays correct text
steps: Verify button text is exactly 'Start Conversion'

features.description: UI elements are properly aligned in columns
steps: Verify labels are left-aligned in first column
Verify entry fields are in second column and expand
Verify Browse buttons are in third column

features.description: Progress bar spans full width of window
steps: Verify progress bar extends across most of the window width
Verify progress bar expands with window

features.description: Status label is centered in window
steps: Verify status label text is centered or left-aligned below progress bar

features.description: Convert button is centered horizontally
steps: Verify 'Start Conversion' button is centered in the window

features.description: UI has consistent padding and spacing
steps: Verify there is consistent padding around UI elements
Verify vertical spacing between rows is uniform

features.description: Application uses system-native styling
steps: Verify buttons, entry fields, and labels match Windows system theme
Verify fonts are readable and appropriate size

features.description: Entry fields show full path text without truncation issues
steps: Select a file with a long path
Verify the entry field displays the path
Scroll or verify full path is visible

features.description: File dialog shows XEF file filter
steps: Verify file type dropdown shows 'XEF files' option
Verify '*.xef' pattern is associated with it

features.description: File dialog shows All files option
steps: Verify file type dropdown includes 'All files' option
Verify '*.  *' pattern is associated with it

features.description: Success dialog shows output directory path
steps: Verify success dialog includes the output directory path

features.description: Error dialogs have appropriate icons
steps: Verify error dialog shows error/warning icon
Verify error dialog shows error icon

features.description: Window can be moved to different screen positions
steps: Verify window stays at new position

features.description: Window can be minimized
Window can be maximized and restored
steps: Verify window returns to original size

features.description: Close button exits application
Keyboard Tab order moves through UI elements logically
steps: Verify focus moves through: input entry, input browse, output entry, output browse, convert button
Verify focused elements show visual indication

features.description: Enter key activates focused button
steps: Verify button action triggers (validation or conversion)

features.description: Application handles corrupted XEF file gracefully
steps: Create a corrupted or invalid .xef file
Attempt to convert the corrupted file
Verify error message indicates file is invalid or corrupted

features.description: Application handles permission denied on output directory
steps: Set output directory to a read-only or protected location
Attempt to convert a valid XEF file

features.description: Application handles disk full scenario
steps: Set output to a nearly full disk (if testable)
Verify application shows appropriate error message

features.description: Application handles network path disconnection
steps: Set input or output to a network path
Verify application shows network error message

features.description: Application handles empty XEF file
steps: Verify application shows appropriate error about empty or invalid file

features.description: Application handles XEF file with no color frames
steps: Use an XEF file containing only depth data
Verify application handles gracefully (error or creates depth-only output)

features.description: Application handles very large XEF file
steps: Use an XEF file larger than 1GB (if available)
Verify application does not crash with out-of-memory

features.description: Application handles Unicode characters in file path
steps: Place XEF file in a folder with Unicode characters in name
Verify conversion succeeds or shows clear error

features.description: Application handles spaces in file path
steps: Place XEF file in a folder with spaces in name
Verify conversion succeeds without path issues

features.description: Application handles long file paths (>260 chars)
steps: Place XEF file in deeply nested folder structure
Verify application handles long path or shows clear error

features.description: Application handles file locked by another process
steps: Open XEF file in another program that locks it
Attempt to convert the locked file

features.description: Application recovers from conversion failure
steps: Verify application returns to ready state

features.description: Multiple rapid conversion attempts don't crash app
steps: Click Convert button rapidly multiple times

features.description: Application handles missing output directory gracefully
steps: Set output to a directory that gets deleted
Verify application shows error about missing directory

features.description: Application handles file with wrong extension
steps: Rename a non-XEF file to have .xef extension

features.description: Unhandled exceptions are caught and shown to user
steps: Review code for try/except blocks around conversion
Verify all conversion errors are caught
Verify errors are shown in messageboxes, not console

features.description: Application does not crash on invalid input during conversion
steps: Verify application never crashes to desktop
Verify application always shows user-friendly error message

features.description: Application launches within 2 seconds
steps: Start application and measure launch time

features.description: File dialog opens within 1 second
steps: Measure time for dialog to appear

features.description: UI remains responsive during conversion
steps: Try to interact with window (move, resize)
Verify window responds to user input

features.description: Conversion completes in reasonable time for typical files
steps: Verify it completes within a reasonable timeframe

features.description: Memory usage stays reasonable during conversion
steps: Start a conversion of a medium-sized file
Verify memory does not grow unbounded
Verify memory is released after conversion

features.description: Large file conversion does not freeze UI
Application starts conversion quickly after button click
Error dialogs appear quickly when triggered
All interactive elements have descriptive text
steps: Inspect all buttons, labels, and entry fields
Verify each has meaningful descriptive text
Verify no generic or missing labels

features.description: Tab navigation covers all interactive elements
steps: Press Tab repeatedly from first element
Verify all buttons and entry fields receive focus

features.description: Focused elements have visible focus indicator
steps: Verify each focused element shows a visible focus rectangle or highlight

features.description: Application works with high contrast mode
steps: Verify UI elements are still visible and readable

features.description: Application text is readable at default DPI
steps: Verify all text is clear and readable
Verify no text is clipped or overlapping

features.description: Application handles high DPI scaling
steps: Verify text is not blurry or misaligned

features.description: Error messages are clear and actionable
steps: Verify each error message clearly describes the problem
Verify each message suggests what the user should do

features.description: Progress indication is visible to colorblind users
steps: Verify progress bar is visible (not relying solely on color)

features.description: Application has minimum touch target sizes for buttons
steps: Verify buttons are at least 32x32 pixels for touch accessibility

features.description: Application works with Windows Narrator
steps: Verify UI elements are announced by Narrator

features.description: Application correctly reads XEF file header
steps: Verify file header is parsed correctly

features.description: Application extracts color frames from XEF
steps: Convert an XEF file with color stream
Verify output JPEG contains color image data
Verify image colors are accurate (not inverted or shifted)

features.description: Application extracts depth frames from XEF
steps: Convert an XEF file with depth stream
Verify output contains depth data representation
Verify depth values are mapped to visual range

features.description: Application handles XEF with both color and depth
steps: Convert an XEF file with both streams
Verify both color and depth outputs are generated
Verify outputs are properly labeled or organized

features.description: JPEG quality is acceptable for color frames
steps: Verify image quality is good (no severe artifacts)

features.description: Output JPEG has standard dimensions
steps: Convert a Kinect V2 color frame
Verify dimensions match Kinect V2 color resolution (1920x1080)

features.description: Depth output has Kinect V2 depth resolution
steps: Verify dimensions match Kinect V2 depth resolution (512x424)

features.description: Frame timestamps are preserved in output naming
steps: Check output file names for timestamp information
Verify timestamps are sequential and reasonable

features.description: Application handles XEF files from different Kinect sensors
steps: Use XEF files from different Kinect V2 devices

features.description: Converted images can be opened in standard image viewers
steps: Open output JPEG in Windows Photos app
Open output JPEG in web browser

features.description: Output directory is created if it doesn't exist
steps: Set output to a non-existent directory
Verify output files are in the new directory

features.description: Existing files in output directory are not overwritten by default
steps: Convert an XEF file to get output
Verify both sets of output exist (or check overwrite behavior)

features.description: Application handles concurrent file selection dialogs
steps: Without closing, try to interact with main window
Verify dialog is modal or behavior is correct

features.description: Application correctly handles Windows path separators
steps: Verify paths work correctly for file operations

features.description: Application works when launched from different directories
steps: Verify default output path still points correctly

features.description: Application handles XEF file with IR stream
steps: Use XEF file with infrared stream data
Verify IR data is converted to grayscale image

features.description: Application provides frame count or progress indication
steps: Verify user can estimate conversion progress

features.description: Cancelled file dialog does not clear existing selection
steps: Click Browse, then Cancel the dialog
Verify input field still shows previously selected file

features.description: Application supports drag and drop of XEF files
steps: Drag a .xef file from Explorer onto application window
Verify file path appears in input field

features.description: Application supports command-line arguments for input file
steps: Verify application launches with file path pre-filled

features.description: Application creates subdirectories for each XEF file
steps: Verify output files are in a 'test' subdirectory

features.description: Application includes frame number in output filename
steps: Verify filenames include frame index (e.g., frame_001.jpg)

features.description: Application handles XEF with variable frame sizes
steps: Use XEF file with frames of different resolutions
Verify output dimensions match source frames

features.description: Application does not produce empty output files
steps: Verify all output JPEG files have non-zero size
Verify all output files contain valid image data

features.description: Application handles XEF with audio stream gracefully
steps: Use XEF file containing audio data
Verify audio data is skipped or handled without error

features.description: Application shows total converted frame count
steps: Verify it indicates how many frames were converted

features.description: Application handles XEF with no frames gracefully
steps: Create or use XEF file with valid header but no frame data
Verify informative error or warning message

features.description: Application preserves aspect ratio in output images
Application creates XEF2JPEG_Output directory if missing
steps: Verify default output path still shows XEF2JPEG_Output
Verify directory is created and files are output

features.description: Application handles multiple conversions in sequence
steps: Change input to second XEF file

features.description: Application output filenames include source XEF name
steps: Verify filenames include 'mysession' or similar identifier

features.description: Application handles XEF with corrupted frame gracefully
steps: Use XEF file with one corrupted frame among valid frames
Verify corrupted frame is skipped with warning or error

features.description: Application output uses standard JPEG format
steps: Verify output files have .jpg or .jpeg extension
Verify files are valid JPEG format (check magic bytes)

features.description: Application handles read-only input file
steps: Set input XEF file to read-only
Verify conversion succeeds (read-only input is fine)

features.description: Application handles input and output on different drives
steps: Set input to C: drive file
Set output to D: drive directory

features.description: Application has consistent color scheme
steps: Verify UI elements use consistent system colors

features.description: Window minimum size prevents layout breaking
steps: Resize window to minimum possible size

features.description: Progress bar shows indeterminate animation
steps: Verify progress bar shows a moving/animated indicator
Verify animation stops when conversion completes

features.description: Error dialogs have OK button to dismiss
steps: Verify dialog has an OK or Close button

features.description: Success dialog has OK button to dismiss
steps: Verify success dialog has OK button

features.description: Application icon is set (if applicable)
steps: Check window icon in title bar
Verify icon is appropriate (not default Python icon)

features.description: Browse button has consistent width
steps: Verify both Browse buttons have same width
Verify button text fits without truncation

features.description: Entry fields have consistent height with buttons
steps: Verify entry fields and buttons are vertically aligned

features.description: Status label is readable and not clipped
Application shows helpful placeholder text in entry fields
steps: Check if entry fields have placeholder or default text
Verify placeholder helps user understand what to enter

features.description: Long file paths are horizontally scrollable in entry
steps: Select a file with a very long path
Use cursor keys to scroll horizontally

features.description: Dialog windows are centered on parent window
steps: Move main window to a corner
Verify dialog appears centered on main window

features.description: Application responds to system theme changes
steps: Verify application adapts or remains functional

features.description: File dialog filter works correctly
Directory dialog opens to current output path
steps: Change output directory entry to custom path
Verify dialog opens to that custom path

features.description: Application uses Pillow for image saving
steps: Verify PIL/Pillow is used for JPEG creation
Verify image.save() is called with JPEG format

features.description: Application reads XEF binary format correctly
steps: Verify binary format is read with correct byte order
Verify frame boundaries are correctly identified

features.description: Application handles little-endian XEF data
steps: Verify XEF parser uses little-endian byte order
Verify output image data is correct

features.description: Application handles different XEF versions
steps: Obtain XEF files from different SDK versions
Verify all versions are supported or show clear errors

features.description: Application converts BGRA color data to RGB for JPEG
steps: Verify BGRA data is converted to RGB before JPEG save
Verify output colors are not inverted or incorrect

features.description: Application maps 16-bit depth to 8-bit grayscale
steps: Verify 16-bit depth values are mapped to 0-255 range

features.description: Application handles Kinect V2 body index data
steps: Use XEF with body index stream
Verify body index is converted or skipped gracefully

features.description: Application handles XEF with skeleton/tracking data
steps: Use XEF with body tracking data
Verify tracking data doesn't cause errors

features.description: Output JPEG compression quality is configurable
steps: Review code for JPEG quality parameter
Verify quality can be adjusted (or is set to reasonable default)
Convert and verify file size reflects quality setting

features.description: Application converts IR stream to grayscale JPEG
steps: Verify IR intensity values are represented

features.description: Application can handle XEF files larger than 4GB
steps: Use an XEF file larger than 4GB
Verify file is read correctly (64-bit file handling)

features.description: Application cleanup resources after conversion
steps: Verify input file can be deleted/moved after conversion

features.description: Application logs conversion activity
steps: Check for log output or log file
Verify key activities are logged (start, frames, completion)

features.description: Application handles Windows path with drive letter
steps: Verify path handling works with drive letters

features.description: Application handles UNC network paths
Application creates output with correct file permissions
steps: Verify files are readable and not locked

features.description: Application handles XEF file with extended metadata
steps: Use XEF file with additional metadata sections
Verify metadata doesn't cause parsing errors

features.description: Application uses threading for non-blocking conversion
steps: Verify conversion happens in background thread

features.description: Application updates progress from background thread
steps: Verify progress bar updates during conversion
Verify updates happen without freezing UI

features.description: Application safely accesses UI from conversion thread
steps: Verify UI updates use proper thread-safe methods
Verify no race conditions in UI updates

features.description: Application handles conversion cancellation
steps: Verify application closes cleanly without crash

features.description: Application validates input before starting conversion
steps: Verify error before any processing starts

features.description: Application uses pathlib for path operations
steps: Review code imports and path handling
Verify pathlib is used for path manipulation
Verify paths are handled correctly across different contexts

features.description: Application has proper error handling in XEF parser
steps: Verify try/except blocks around parsing operations
Verify errors are propagated to UI with clear messages

features.description: Application validates XEF file magic bytes
steps: Attempt to convert non-XEF file with .xef extension
Verify application checks file format signature

features.description: Application handles XEF stream configuration data
steps: Parse XEF file with stream configuration
Verify resolution, format, and fps are read correctly

features.description: Application writes JPEG with correct EXIF orientation
Application uses context managers for file operations
steps: Verify 'with' statements are used for file operations
Verify files are properly closed after operations

features.description: Application handles system sleep/wake during conversion
steps: Verify conversion completes or shows appropriate status

features.description: Application supports batch conversion of multiple files
Application includes version information
steps: Check for version display or about dialog

features.description: Application gracefully handles import errors
steps: Verify user-friendly error message is shown

features.description: Application does not modify input XEF file
steps: Calculate checksum of input XEF file

features.description: Application handles very short XEF file (1-2 frames)
steps: Use XEF file with only 1-2 frames
Verify correct number of output files

features.description: Application handles XEF with interleaved streams
steps: Use XEF with color and depth frames interleaved
Verify both stream types are correctly separated

features.description: Application uses appropriate buffer sizes for reading
steps: Verify efficient buffer sizes are used
Verify no excessive memory allocation for reads

features.description: Application converts depth data to proper grayscale values
steps: Verify closer objects appear brighter (or darker, consistently)

features.description: Application handles XEF with no color stream gracefully
steps: Use XEF with only depth and IR streams
Verify conversion succeeds or shows clear error

features.description: Application handles large number of frames (>1000)
steps: Use XEF file with many frames

features.description: Application has clean exit on Ctrl+C
Application main window is resizable with minimum size
steps: Attempt to resize window very small
Verify window has minimum size limit
Verify UI elements remain usable at minimum size

features.description: Application restores window position on relaunch
steps: Verify window appears at same position

features.description: Application output filenames are Windows-compatible
steps: Verify output filenames don't contain invalid characters
Verify filenames don't use reserved names (CON, PRN, etc.)

features.description: Application handles simultaneous Browse button clicks
Application includes error traceback in log for debugging
steps: Verify traceback is available for debugging

features.description: Application initializes tkinter properly
steps: Verify tk.Tk() is created before XEF2JPEGApp

features.description: Application uses StringVar correctly for entry binding
steps: Verify StringVar is used for input_file and output_directory
Verify entry fields are bound to StringVars

features.description: Application correctly uses grid layout manager
steps: Verify row/column weights are set for resizing

features.description: Application sets window size via geometry
steps: Verify window size matches specified geometry

features.description: Application uses ttk widgets for modern appearance
steps: Verify ttk.Button, ttk.Entry, ttk.Label are used
Verify widgets have native Windows appearance

features.description: Application handles file dialog parent window correctly
steps: Verify dialog is modal to main window
Verify main window cannot be interacted with while dialog is open

features.description: Application uses os.path.exists for file validation
steps: Verify os.path.exists or pathlib.exists is used
Verify validation happens before conversion starts

features.description: Application converts frame data to PIL Image correctly
steps: Verify numpy array or bytes are converted to PIL Image
Verify Image.frombuffer or Image.fromarray is used correctly

features.description: Application saves JPEG with correct mode (RGB/L)
steps: Verify color images are saved in RGB mode
Verify depth images are saved in L (grayscale) mode

features.description: Application generates unique output filenames
steps: Convert same XEF file twice to different output dirs
Verify no filename collisions when converting multiple files

features.description: Application handles Windows file locking correctly
steps: Try to delete output file while conversion is writing
Verify appropriate error or successful completion

features.description: Application works with Python 3.8+
Application handles missing tkinter gracefully
steps: Test on minimal Python install without tkinter
Verify clear error message about missing tkinter

features.description: Application properly terminates on window close
steps: Verify process terminates in task manager

features.description: Application handles rapid open/close of file dialogs
Application uses consistent error handling patterns
steps: Verify errors are shown via messagebox.showerror

features.description: Application uses messagebox.showinfo for success
Application uses messagebox.showerror for errors
steps: Verify messagebox.showerror is used for all error dialogs
Verify error title and message are set

features.description: Application disables button during conversion to prevent double-click
steps: Verify button cannot be clicked during conversion

features.description: Application starts progress bar animation during conversion
steps: Verify progress.stop() is called after conversion

features.description: Application uses after() for UI updates from threads
steps: Verify root.after() is used for UI updates
Verify no direct UI manipulation from worker thread

features.description: Application has proper __name__ == '__main__' guard
steps: Verify main() is called within guard

features.description: Application class is properly structured with __init__
Application stores state variables as instance attributes
steps: Verify input_file, output_directory are instance attributes

features.description: Application uses default output path from current directory
steps: Verify output directory default is cwd/XEF2JPEG_Output
Verify path uses Path.cwd() or equivalent
